breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome Author Publishes Strawman Playbook — While Conceding Our Core Critiques
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: The dome author released review-response.html labeling five "strawman critique patterns" — but the same commit reclassified WIN-013/014 as "supportive_nonunique" and WIN-058 as "pending_contested", exactly the buckets our review said they belonged in. The strawman label and the concession arrived in the same commit.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: The dome author published a defensive page claiming our arguments are strawmen while simultaneously reclassifying WINs in the direction our review demanded. This is a newsworthy pattern worth documenting.

breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome Own Taxonomy Lands Zero of Six Classified WINs in "Prospective" Column
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: The dome author published a five-class claim taxonomy where only prospective_confirmed items are genuine forecasts. Of the six WINs he has classified so far in his own partial registry, not one is prospective_confirmed — the only item with that tag is a pending (unconfirmed) eclipse prediction.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: The dome author self-applied the key discriminating criterion (prospective vs retrospective) and his own result confirms our review position. 63 of 69 WINs remain unclassified.
